diagrams.title: Dropout Mechanism in Neural Networks
diagrams.description: Visualizes the dropout regularization technique where random neurons are temporarily removed during training to prevent overfitting. Shows a neural network with some neurons randomly deactivated (crossed out or faded) while others remain active.
elements: Training mode vs Inference mode labels
diagrams.purpose: Explains how dropout randomly deactivates neurons during each training iteration to improve network generalization and prevent co-adaptation of neurons.

diagrams.title: Batch Normalization Process
diagrams.description: Shows the batch normalization operation: normalizing activations within each mini-batch to zero mean and unit variance, then applying learnable scale (gamma) and shift (beta) parameters.
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates how batch normalization stabilizes training by normalizing layer inputs, reducing internal covariate shift and enabling higher learning rates.

diagrams.title: Layer Architecture: Dropout and Batch Normalization Placement
diagrams.description: Illustrates the typical ordering of dropout and batch normalization within a neural network layer, showing the data flow from input through transformation, normalization, activation, and regularization.
diagrams.purpose: Shows how dropout and batch normalization are integrated into a standard feedforward/convolutional layer, clarifying their typical placement for optimal performance.
